danger_signals.visual_form: Red liquid imagery (censored to green 'slime' in US release)
danger_signals.meaning: Presence of danger, death, horror environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Abstract black-and-pink patterns, dark atmospheric backgrounds
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile/dangerous realm distinct from safe world

danger_signals.visual_form: Doorways leading to isolated boss arenas
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous encounters ahead; many doors lead to empty rooms (deception/uncertainty)

danger_signals.visual_form: Horror-derived creature designs (mummies, spiders, zombies, Medusa, Grim Reaper, possessed objects)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hostility; threats from multiple sources

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual patterns of incoming fire from enemies (musical notes, shrimp projectiles, beam attacks)

danger_signals.meaning: Damage taken; health is critical resource

safety_signals.visual_form: Heart sprites appearing after enemy defeat
Pill items trigger visual/sprite change to winged griffin-like form
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up state; temporary invulnerability and enhanced offensive capability (flight, beam attacks)

safety_signals.visual_form: Keys appear after boss defeat; status bar displays key indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Level progression unlocked; boss threat eliminated
Potential reward access (health, points, or transformation)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Normal human child form vs. Bert transformation (winged, beam-wielding)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Baseball game origin establishes mundane world contrast

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Griffin-like winged form; beam attack projection
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fused with Mark; appears as temporary power-up state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Represents Dark World authority; requests human aid against monsters

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Horror icon parodies (Medusa, Grim Reaper, mummy, dragon, etc.)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss forms; copyrighted character parodies modified for US release
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Abstract dark backgrounds; monster population; 'Dark World' realm designation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Princess figure (deceptive beauty transforming to monster)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Apparent safe realm that reveals as threat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ascent from Dark World; initially appears as reward destination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black-pink abstract patterns; elaborate horror-themed enemy designs; evil presence established through creature variety
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively attack; bosses require defeat to progress; monsters populate each level doorway

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Baseball game context; mundane human child protagonist; domestic setting (bedroom, school path implied)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mark is reluctant helper; initially unprepared for danger; dream-like awakening reversal at game end

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green slime replacing blood imagery; copyrighted boss characters blacked out; simplified background aesthetics vs. prototype
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Word 'hell' retained despite Nintendo censorship policies; invisible collision data persists where sprites removed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mark changes to griffin-winged form with beam projection capability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced offensive power; ability to fly; limited duration requiring resource management

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Apparent celestial/safe environment contrasting Dark World
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Deceptive realm; princess transforms to monster; leads to ambiguous ending (dream vs. reality)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Contrast between visual prototype and final release
environmental_storytelling.example: Original mountain range sunset replaced with abstract black-pink; blood imagery censored to green slime; elaborate enemy designs simplified; signals localization/content removal narrative
Little Shop of Horrors plant boss (microphone/speaker) becomes generic plant; Statue of Liberty/Planet of the Apes boss becomes happy pumpkin-ghost; signals content control/legal pressure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Horror genre homage through enemy archetypes
environmental_storytelling.example: Mummy, spiders, zombies, Medusa, dragon, Grim Reaper establish Dark World as horror genre anthology; Japanese folklore references (Jinmenken, Banchō Sarayashiki) add cultural specificity
Walking pants, giant cat hurling kittens, bouncing fried shrimp, possessed furniture communicate absurdist horror; unsettling normality-inversion
Many doors lead to empty rooms while others contain bosses; visual design does not distinguish threats; signals unpredictability and danger

environmental_storytelling.technique: Palette and background design as realm identifier
environmental_storytelling.example: Dark World uses abstract black-pink; normal world uses mundane baseball context; Heavenly realm distinct through princess imagery

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Uses baseball bat defensively; limited offensive options; must rebound projectiles to damage bosses; hesitant about Dark World involvement
Flies; projects beam attacks; stronger offensive capability; limited duration requiring pill resource acquisition
Initiates adventure; fuses with Mark; remains fused throughout game; returns Mark home after Dark World cleared; reappears at game end
Projectile attacks; parody-based attack patterns; require defeat for key acquisition; vary by room/level
Drop hearts upon defeat; signal successful combat; indicate health recovery opportunity
Appear consistently in doorways; provide random health, points, or transformation pills; create uncertainty/risk-reward dynamic
Initially appears beautiful; transforms to hideous monster; melts Mark's flesh; signals betrayal and realm inversion

notes: Monster Party is a surreal adventure game with unusual visual communication strategy. Core mechanic: danger/safety contrast through enemy variety, projectile reflection requirements, and power-up transformation. Faction structure is minimal (Mark vs. Dark World monsters); primary tension is between mundane human world and hostile alien realm. Visual censorship (blood→slime, copyrighted characters→generic parodies) is itself a storytelling element, as prototype content reveals original darker intent. Game's bizarre premise (baseball-bat-wielding child enlisted by alien to fight horror archetypes) communicates world-state primarily through enemy design and background abstraction rather than explicit UI or narrative text. Ending ambiguity (dream vs. reality) suggests unreliable world-state perception. T3 data: qualitative observations based on source documentation; no quantitative metrics available.
